question: Q1
claim: aionforge models currentness as edge-presence + closed validity windows (valid_from/valid_to/ingested_at/expired_at) on the edge, supersession closes old window and writes SUPERSEDED_BY, never deletes

question: Q1
claim: five physical DELETE-FROM-code_edges sites confirmed in code-graph (no temporal columns; currentness = physical presence)

question: Q1
title: add valid_at/invalid_at columns to code_edges; supersede instead of delete on reindex

question: Q1
title: represent path-coupled-ID renames as SUPERSEDES edges instead of delete+create
note: keyed on matching contentHash across rename; strictly additive; composes with Q1-C1; lower-risk alternative to Q2-C2

question: Q2
claim: aionforge IDs are content-addressed and idempotent (fact_id hashes triple+episode+rule_version; entity id hashes namespace+type+normalized_name) which makes replay a no-op

question: Q2
claim: code-graph edge IDs are NOT content-addressed; source_id/target_id are path-coupled symbol IDs so a path-changing rename looks like delete+create

question: Q2
claim: parser skip-list does NOT classify transient vs fatal; B1/B2/OTHER are crash cohorts; no max_retries; removal is permanent no-op (recordSuccess deprecated empty, no self-heal) so a crashing file is permanently skipped

question: Q2
claim: aionforge quarantine: PassError::Transient leaves episode raw (retries, cursor holds); PassError::Fatal or transient-exceeds-max_retries(5) marks failed (retained, audited, skipped, pipeline proceeds); attempt count from durable failed-audit not memory

question: Q2
title: add Transient/Fatal classification + bounded retry (max_retries default 5) to parser skip-list, reusing durable attempt_count
conflictWith: current must-not-auto-unskip / no-self-heal stance parser-skip-list.ts:93-97 — deliberate policy change, needs owner sign-off
